title: Module Check: Tree and Graph Traversal — BFS/DFS Duality

questions.question: In DFS three-colour marking, what do white, gray, and black represent?
questions.answer: White: unvisited. Gray: on the current DFS call stack. Black: fully processed.

questions.question: Why does BFS guarantee shortest path in an unweighted graph?
questions.answer: BFS explores all nodes at distance d before any at d+1 — the first reach is via the minimum number of edges

questions.question: After Kahn's topological sort, `len(result) < n`. What does this mean?
questions.answer: The graph contains a directed cycle

questions.question: Which traversal processes children before the parent? Give one problem where this is required.
questions.answer: Post-order DFS. Example: maximum depth of tree, tree diameter, serialise/deserialise tree
